segments.text: And the children of Israel set forward, and pitched in the plains of Moab on this side Jordan by Jericho.
And Balak the son of Zippor saw all that Israel had done to the Amorites.
And Moab was sore afraid of the people, because they were many: and Moab was distressed because of the children of Israel.
Now shall this company lick up all that are round about us, as the ox licketh up the grass of the field.
segments.speaker: Moab

segments.text: And Balak the son of Zippor was king of the Moabites at that time.
He sent messengers therefore unto Balaam the son of Beor to Pethor, which is by the river of the land of the children of his people, to call him, saying,
Behold, there is a people come out from Egypt: behold, they cover the face of the earth, and they abide over against me:
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: Come now therefore, I pray thee, curse me this people; for they are too mighty for me: peradventure I shall prevail, that we may smite them, and that I may drive them out of the land: for I wot that he whom thou blessest is blessed, and he whom thou cursest is cursed.
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: And the elders of Moab and the elders of Midian departed with the rewards of divination in their hand; and they came unto Balaam, and spake unto him the words of Balak.
Lodge here this night, and I will bring you word again, as the LORD shall speak unto me:
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: and the princes of Moab abode with Balaam.
What men are these with thee?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Balak the son of Zippor, king of Moab, hath sent unto me, saying,
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Behold, there is a people come out of Egypt, which covereth the face of the earth: come now, curse me them; peradventure I shall be able to overcome them, and drive them out.
segments.speaker: Balaam

footnotes: I shall…: Heb. I shall prevail in fighting against him

segments.text: Thou shalt not go with them; thou shalt not curse the people: for they are blessed.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Get you into your land: for the LORD refuseth to give me leave to go with you.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: And the princes of Moab rose up, and they went unto Balak, and said, Balaam refuseth to come with us.
And Balak sent yet again princes, more, and more honourable than they.
Thus saith Balak the son of Zippor, Let nothing, I pray thee, hinder thee from coming unto me:
segments.speaker: Balak's Princes

footnotes: Let…: Heb. Be not thou hindered from, etc

segments.text: For I will promote thee unto very great honour, and I will do whatsoever thou sayest unto me: come therefore, I pray thee, curse me this people.
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: If Balak would give me his house full of silver and gold, I cannot go beyond the word of the LORD my God, to do less or more.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Now therefore, I pray you, tarry ye also here this night, that I may know what the LORD will say unto me more.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: If the men come to call thee, rise up, and go with them; but yet the word which I shall say unto thee, that shalt thou do.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And Balaam rose up in the morning, and saddled his ass, and went with the princes of Moab.
And God's anger was kindled because he went: and the angel of the LORD stood in the way for an adversary against him. Now he was riding upon his ass, and his two servants were with him.
What have I done unto thee, that thou hast smitten me these three times?
segments.speaker: The Ass

segments.text: Because thou hast mocked me: I would there were a sword in mine hand, for now would I kill thee.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Am not I thine ass, upon which thou hast ridden ever since I was thine unto this day? was I ever wont to do so unto thee?
segments.speaker: The Ass

segments.text: Nay.
segments.speaker: Balaam

footnotes: upon…: Heb. who hast ridden upon me
ever since…: or, ever since thou wast, etc

segments.text: Lo, I am come unto thee: have I now any power at all to say any thing? the word that God putteth in my mouth, that shall I speak.
segments.speaker: Balaam
